examples.title: Procedural Programming Example - Student Management
examples.description: C program demonstrating procedural approach to manage student records using functions and structures
examples.explanation: This procedural C program manages student records using functions. Data (struct Student) and functions are separate. The main() function controls program flow by calling functions in sequence.

examples.title: OOP Example - Student Class with Encapsulation
examples.description: C++ program demonstrating OOP principles: encapsulation, constructor, and member functions
examples.explanation: This C++ program demonstrates OOP with class Student. Private data members (name, rollNo, marks) are hidden from outside access. Public methods provide controlled access - this is encapsulation. Constructors initialize objects, and the destructor cleans up.

examples.title: Inheritance and Polymorphism Example
examples.description: C++ program demonstrating inheritance hierarchy with base class Shape and derived classes Circle and Rectangle showing polymorphism
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates inheritance (Circle and Rectangle inherit from Shape) and polymorphism (virtual function area() behaves differently for each shape). The base class pointer can call derived class methods - runtime polymorphism. Pure virtual function makes Shape abstract.

examples.title: Multiple Inheritance Example
examples.description: C++ program demonstrating multiple inheritance with base classes Person and Employee
examples.explanation: This demonstrates multiple inheritance where Manager class inherits from both Person and Employee. Manager gets properties from both base classes. In practice, multiple inheritance can cause ambiguity (diamond problem), which is solved using virtual base classes in C++.
